rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component imino-trifluoromethylation of olefins using Langlois reagent (CF3SO2Na) and benzophenone imines as bis-nucleophilic CF3 and amine sources, providing trifluoromethylated primary amine precursors.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent) generates CF3 radical with loss of SO2; the benzyl radical formed after CF3 addition to the olefin is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl cation that is trapped by the imine nitrogen; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzyl radical after CF3 addition to styrene
iminium / N-attack adduct leading to C=N product
α-amino C-radical (Path b alternative: benzyl radical adds to C=N of imine)

electron_flow: CF3SO2- is preferentially oxidized at the anode (Eox ~1.39 V vs olefin ~1.63 V; mixture shows first peak at 1.27 V), extruding SO2 to give CF3•, which adds to the styrene to form a benzyl radical; this radical is oxidized at the anode to a benzyl cation captured by the imine nitrogen to form the C–N bond, with cathodic H+ reduction to H2.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of CF3SO2Na / styrene / imine combinations
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(CF3SO2Na)=1.39 V; Eox(olefin)≈1.63 V; mixture shows first oxidation at 1.27 V, indicating CF3SO2- is oxidized preferentially

probe_reagents.name: Hydrolysis derivatization (PPTS, THF/H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Imine 5a hydrolyzed to β-CF3 primary amine tosylate 6 in 82% yield, supporting imine intermediate

probe_reagents.name: LiAlH4 reduction attempt
probe_reagents.observation: Failed to reduce C=N to secondary amine (steric hindrance), indirectly supporting imine structure

notes: Authors favor Path a (radical–polar crossover: CF3• addition, anodic oxidation of benzyl radical to cation, then nucleophilic capture by imine). An alternative Path b is proposed but not excluded: the benzyl radical adds directly to the C=N bond of the imine to give an aminyl/α-amino radical, which is then oxidized at the anode with loss of an N–H proton to regenerate the C=N. Single-crystal X-ray (CCDC 2456921) confirms product structure.
